name: Dark Christmas Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Candied cherries, cut in half
recipeInstructions:
This recipe makes a very large amount (about 28 cups) of fruit cake batter and can be baked in various sizes of pans. For small cakes for gifts, divide the batter among 9 8x4x3 inch loaf pans using generous 3 cups of batter each. For larger loaf pans, use 6 9x5x3 inch pans with a generous 4 1/2 cups of batter in each. Three 12 cup Bundt pans require generous 9 cups of batter per pan. Two cakes can be made in 10-inch tube pans using 12 cups of batter for each and one 8x4x2 1/2 inch loaf pan with the remaining batter. Any combination of these sizes is possible.
Line greased pans (except Bundt pan) with greased heavy brown paper. Grease and flour the Bundt pan.
Combine nuts and fruit in a very large bowl or roasting pan. Add 1 cup flour and toss to coat fruit. Cream butter until smooth using an electric mixer. Add sugar and beat until fluffy. Beat in egg yolks. Transfer mixture to a large bowl. Sift together 5 1/2 cups flour, salt, spices, and baking soda. Alternately add prune juice and brandy with the sifted ingredients. Mix well.
Fold in beaten egg whites. Pour into prepared pans and press well into corners. Preheat oven to 275°F. Bake 8x4x3 inch pans for about 3 hours, 9x5x3 inch or Bundt pans for about 3 hours and 15 minutes, and 10-inch tube pans for about 3 1/2 hours until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow to cool on racks before unwrapping and wrapping cakes in cheesecloth dampened with brandy and then foil. Store in a cool place. Makes 15 lbs of fruit cake.
